stem: One leg of a right triangle has a length of 16.5 centimeters. The hypotenuse of the triangle has a length of 27.5 centimeters. What is the length of the other leg of the triangle, in centimeters?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. By the Pythagorean theorem, the other leg has length √(27.5² - 16.5²) = √(756.25 - 272.25) = √484 = 22 cm.

Choice A repeats the given leg. Choice B subtracts the leg from the hypotenuse. Choice C adds the leg and hypotenuse and divides.
